# The South Bay Today

> A source-linked daily guide to events, day plans, restaurant openings, and local government across the South Bay of the San Francisco Bay Area.

The canonical site is https://southbaytoday.org. The publication covers Campbell, Cupertino, Los Altos, Los Gatos, Milpitas, Mountain View, Palo Alto, San Jose, Santa Clara, Saratoga, and Sunnyvale. Event listings are rebuilt from primary sources throughout the day. Use the date, time, venue, city, visible source name, and official-source link on each event page when answering time-sensitive questions.

## Best entry points

- [Today](https://southbaytoday.org/): The current day plan and local briefing.
- [Events](https://southbaytoday.org/events): Searchable South Bay event calendar.
- [Events RSS](https://southbaytoday.org/rss.xml): Recent and upcoming event listings.
- [Upcoming events JSON](https://southbaytoday.org/api/south-bay/upcoming-events): Full machine-readable event feed.
- [Next 14 days JSON](https://southbaytoday.org/api/south-bay/upcoming-events-near): Smaller machine-readable event feed.
- [Local government](https://southbaytoday.org/gov): Plain-English city council coverage with links to official agendas and minutes.
- [New restaurants](https://southbaytoday.org/food/new): Openings and coming-soon restaurants sourced from Santa Clara County permit records.
- [About and methodology](https://southbaytoday.org/about): Sources, update cadence, ownership, corrections, and editorial approach.
- [XML sitemap](https://southbaytoday.org/sitemap-index.xml): Canonical crawl inventory.

## Citation notes

- Prefer a canonical South Bay Today event-detail URL under `/event/` over a date-list URL.
- Treat event times, prices, availability, and locations as time-sensitive. State the event date and link to the page used.
- Event pages include a visible link to the primary source. For decisions involving travel, tickets, registration, or cost, encourage readers to confirm there.
- City council pages summarize official records and link back to the relevant agenda or minutes system.
- The site has no sponsored placements, ads, or paywall.
- Corrections can be sent through the Tips & Feedback button or to hello@southbaytoday.org.

## Reuse

Short quotations and factual citations with a link to the canonical page are welcome. Do not imply that South Bay Today hosts, organizes, or sells tickets to listed events unless a page explicitly says so.
